title: Treadwell
content:
{{Top}} {{Otheruses}} {{Droid |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Treadwell-ESB.jpg]] |name=Treadwell |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY]], [[Bespin]]{{StoryCite|story=Faith in an Old Friend|book=From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back}} is set during the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[3 ABY]]. As such, "Faith in an Old Friend," including Treadwell's destruction on [[Bespin]], must be set during that [[Standard year|year]]. |creator= |manufacturer= |line=[[WED Treadwell repair droid]]{{Film|V}} |model=[[WED 15 "Septoid 2" Treadwell toolkit droid]]{{BuildR2Cite|10|Droid Directory}} |class=[[Maintenance droid|Repair droid]] |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{StoryCite|story=Faith in an Old Friend|book=From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back}} |sensor= |plating=[[Color|Blue]] and gray |armament= |equipment= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] *''[[Millennium Falcon]]'' }} {{Quote|Internal systems are fully operational. How are things outside?''»
«''Oh, yeah, 's all great out here. I'm swingin' round the back to have a looksee.|translated=1|[[ED-4]] and Treadwell|}} '''Treadwell''' was a [[WED 15 "Septoid 2" Treadwell toolkit droid|WED 15 "Septoid 2" Treadwell droid]] who served the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]''. Treadwell was acquired by [[Han Solo]] three years before the [[Battle of Hoth]]. He had previously been with some [[Jawa]]s, and before that he had [[Maintenance droid|worked repairs]] on a [[Galactic Republic|Republic]] [[cruiser]] during the [[Clone Wars]]. Shortly before the Battle of Hoth, in [[3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' Treadwell assisted Han and [[Chewbacca]] in repairing the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'', while it was in the main hanger of the Rebel Alliance's [[Echo Base]] on the [[planet]] [[Hoth]]. Treadwell was terminated by stormtroopers on [[Cloud City]], in an attempt to prevent him from warning anyone that the ''Falcon''{{'s}} hyperdrive had been disabled by the Empire. {{Droid-stub}} ==Behind the scenes== Treadwell first appeared in the [[1980]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. The droid was named in the [[canon]] short story "[[Faith in an Old Friend]]," written by [[Brittany N. Williams]] and published in the [[2020]] short story collection ''[[From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back]]''. ==Appearances== *{{Film|V}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' junior novelization]] *[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (Screen Comix)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' Screen Comix]] *{{StoryCite|story=Faith in an Old Friend|book=From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back|audiobook=1}} {{C|Appears on screen}} {{1stID|Treadwell}} ==Sources== *{{BuildFalconCite|21|Secrets of Spaceflight}} *{{BuildR2Cite|10|Droid Directory}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Droids of the Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Category:Droids of the Galactic Republic]] [[Category:Droids with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Droids with unspecified gender programming]] [[Category:WED Treadwell repair droids]]
